7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L24",null,{"children":["$","$25",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@26"}]}]
9:["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most desk lamps sold are not good desk lamps. They provide a single fixed colour temperature, no brightness adjustment, and a design that directs light into your eyes rather than onto the work surface."}}]
a:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A quality desk lamp does three things: it provides adjustable brightness, it provides adjustable colour temperature (warm to cool), and it directs light onto your work rather than at your eyes or your monitor."}}]
b:["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For home office use, there is a fourth consideration: how does it look on camera? Your desk lamp appears in every video call. A lamp with good build quality and thoughtful design makes the workspace look professional."}}]
c:["$","h2","4",{"id":"what-to-look-for","children":"What to Look For"}]
d:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Adjustable colour temperature</strong>: The ability to shift between warm (2700K) and cool (6000K) light lets you optimise for different tasks and times of day. Warm light is easier on the eyes in the evening; cool light improves alertness for detailed morning work."}}]
e:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Adjustable brightness</strong>: At minimum, three brightness levels. Stepless adjustment is better."}}]
f:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>CRI 90+</strong>: High Colour Rendering Index means the light renders colours accurately and feels natural. Low CRI produces a slightly off colour cast that contributes to fatigue."}}]
10:["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Flicker-free</strong>: Cheap LEDs flicker at imperceptible frequencies that still contribute to eye strain and headaches. Quality lamps specify flicker-free operation."}}]
11:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Positioning</strong>: The arm should allow positioning the light source to the side and above the work surface — not directly overhead (creates harsh shadows) and not pointed at the screen (creates glare)."}}]
12:["$","h2","10",{"id":"best-desk-lamps-in-2026","children":"Best Desk Lamps in 2026"}]
13:["$","h3","11",{"id":"best-overall-benq-e-reading-lamp-90","children":"Best Overall: BenQ e-Reading Lamp (~£90)"}]
14:["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The most thoughtful design for computer desk use. Positions the light source above the monitor, illuminating the desk surface without causing any glare on the screen. Adjustable colour temperature and brightness, high CRI, flicker-free. The asymmetric beam design specifically targets desk surfaces rather than eyes."}}]
15:["$","h3","13",{"id":"best-monitor-clip-benq-screenbar-100","children":"Best Monitor Clip: BenQ ScreenBar (~£100)"}]
16:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Clips directly to the top of your monitor, eliminating the lamp base from the desk surface entirely. Auto-dimming sensor adjusts brightness based on ambient light. The ScreenBar Pro (~£160) adds a wireless controller. The cleanest desk lighting solution available for single-monitor setups."}}]
17:["$","h3","15",{"id":"best-smart-lamp-elgato-key-light-130","children":"Best Smart Lamp: Elgato Key Light (~£130)"}]
18:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Originally designed for content creators, the Key Light produces exceptionally high-quality light optimised for video. Mounted on a desk stand or arm, it acts as both a desk lamp and a key light for video calls. Controlled via an app or the Stream Deck. The best choice if you do significant video calling."}}]
19:["$","h3","17",{"id":"best-budget-taotronics-led-desk-lamp-35","children":"Best Budget: TaoTronics LED Desk Lamp (~£35)"}]
1a:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Five colour temperature modes, five brightness levels, a USB charging port, and a memory function. Not as refined as the BenQ options but provides all the functional requirements at a fraction of the price."}}]
1b:["$","h3","19",{"id":"best-design-anglepoise-original-1227-150","children":"Best Design: Anglepoise Original 1227 (~£150)"}]
1c:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The design classic. Spring-balanced arm, precise positioning, and a quality that suggests it will still be on your desk in 30 years. Pair with a Philips Hue smart bulb for adjustable colour temperature. The right choice if aesthetics matter as much as function."}}]
1d:["$","h2","21",{"id":"positioning-for-minimum-eye-strain","children":"Positioning for Minimum Eye Strain"}]
1e:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Place the lamp to the side of your monitor — not behind it, not in front of it. The light should illuminate your hands and any physical reference materials without being visible in your peripheral vision or reflected in the screen."}}]
1f:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For a full guide on home office lighting principles, read our <a href=\"/blog/home-office-lighting-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">home office lighting guide</a>."}}]
